“Well, it depends. It’s been a long time since I’ve had an LP on a coin that flatlines, or at least I would have gotten out before it flatlines most of the time. If it flatlines, it means you’re too late. It means the rug already happened. You should have gotten out way before that.”

🚨 Solana Co-Founder @toly deploys an immutable Percolator binary with admin keys burned, challenging devs to hack it and claim ~5 SOL

The experiment turns security into an open competition, inviting builders to break a live risk engine with no upgrade path.
Yakovenko even suggests using AI for the exploit — with potential rewards including funding or job opportunities for successful attackers.

Tether froze $344M USDT on Tron, one of the largest compliance actions in its history.

Two addresses flagged:

- TNiq9…QZH81: ~$213M
- TTiDL…pjSr9: ~$131M

Executed with OFAC + multiple U.S. law enforcement agencies. Allegedly tied to sanctions evasion and criminal networks.

> Tether has now frozen over $4.4 BILLION in assets and works with 340 law enforcement agencies in 65 countries.

Just weeks after Circle declined to freeze $230M stolen in the $285M Drift Protocol exploit, citing no formal OFAC request even as funds moved through CCTP during U.S. business hours.

Tether stepped in instead, committing $127.5M to Drift's recovery. Drift has since switched to USDT as primary settlement on Solana.

Lifetime tally:
- $4.4B frozen
- 2,300+ cases supported
- 340 agencies across 65 countries
- $2.1B tied directly to U.S. authorities

Paolo Ardoino: "USDT is not a safe haven for illicit activity."

Thoughts?

In just one month we've seen over $500M in losses due to exploits

↳ Apr 1 — Drift: $285M — admin key taken over
↳ Apr 18 — KelpDAO: $293M — LayerZero messages forged
↳ Apr 16 — Grinex: $15M — hot wallet breached
↳ Apr 16 — Rhea Lend: $7.6M — fake collateral accepted
↳ Apr 12 — Hyperbridge: $2.5M — cross-chain message forged
↳ Apr 4 — BSC TMM/USDT: $1.67M — reserves manipulated
↳ Apr 9 — Aethir: $423K — access control bypassed
↳ Apr 13 — Dango: $410K — logic flaw exploited
↳ Apr 3 — Silo V2: $392K — oracle manipulated

Here's the thing tho, this wasn't sophisticated smart contract wizardry. No complex code exploits.

Reject modernity. Return to open source immutable software. I am actually more bullish on being able to remove admin keys than any other time in my professional software development career.  The tools we have for formal verification are sci fi. 

https://t.co/Xv4mMeKtxE

I have deployed an immutable binary of percolator, and a market with about 5 sol in the insurance vault with all the admin keys burned.  There is no way for me to modify anything about the market, or the program.  Anyone can crank it if they choose to.  

It is purely a risk engine and cannot trade or offer prices on its own.  For that you need to figure out how to deploy your own program to do it. 

Figure out how to hack it or manipulate the engine and steal the 5 sol, and I will do my best to land you a job or an angel investment.  Especially if you can figure out how to get AI to hack it instead of doing it yourself.  

This has been my security attack search script if you need a starting place.

https://t.co/WdRwJMKrHL

Find a bug, submit an issue to the repo.  Good luck!
